deconstruction_targets: the concept that value is inherent in use values (coats, linen)
the idea that labour has intrinsic worth based on its productive relation to specific commodities
the assumption that different types of labour are qualitatively distinct in their contribution to value
summary: The passage deconstructs the notion of value by distinguishing between use value and exchange value, arguing that commodity value derives solely from abstract human labour regardless of concrete form or productive differences. It dismantles the idea that different labours (e.g., tailoring vs weaving) have inherent qualitative differences in value creation, exposing their reduction to homogeneous human labour power. The rhetoric is analytically intense with moderate moral framing, focusing on systemic contradictions rather than individual blame.
